evidence.claim: Replacement Cost Value (RCV) coverage pays to rebuild at current prices; Actual Cash Value (ACV) pays depreciated value — major difference on older homes / roofs.
Standard homeowner policies typically exclude: flood (need NFIP or private flood), earthquake, mold above caps, sewer backup, wind in some coastal areas.

criteria.name: dwelling_coverage
criteria.notes: Should match rebuild cost, not market price.

criteria.name: rcv_vs_acv
criteria.notes: RCV pays at current prices; ACV pays depreciated (E1).

confabulationPatterns.reality: Standard policies exclude flood, earthquake, and often sewer backup or mold (E2). 'Comprehensive' is a feel-word with no defined meaning in this category.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the policy include flood, earthquake, sewer backup, and mold? These are commonly excluded.

confabulationPatterns.reality: RCV covers dwelling/contents at current prices but typically caps at policy limit. If the limit doesn't match actual rebuild cost, the homeowner eats the gap. Get a current rebuild estimate (E1).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Has the dwelling coverage limit been set to match a current rebuild estimate (not market value)?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Bundling discounts vary; the same carrier may offer 5-25%. Always quote standalone home + auto separately and compare to bundled.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Has the user compared bundled vs standalone quotes from multiple carriers?

compatibilityQuestions: Flood risk? Standard policy does NOT cover flood (E2). FEMA zones require NFIP or private flood policy.
Wildfire / hurricane area? Some carriers are non-renewing policies in California, Florida, Louisiana.
High-value items (jewelry, art, instruments)? May need scheduled personal property rider.
